title: Buttery Silky Low Fat Italian Wild Garlic Leaves Stir-fry
description: A weeknight Ligurian-inspired dish featuring wild garlic leaves in a buttery, silky sauce made with low-fat ingredients.
instructions: Clean and chop the wild garlic leaves into thin strips. Wash and cut broccoli rabe into small florets.
Heat 3 tablespoons of olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic cloves, stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chopped wild garlic leaves to the pan with some salt, cook for another 2-3 minutes until they start to wilt slightly but not too soft.
Stir in red wine vinegar if using, and let it reduce by half (about 5 minutes).
Deglaze the wok with low-sodium chicken broth or water, bringing to a simmer. Add salt and pepper to taste, mix well.
Turn down heat to medium-low. Stir in low-fat butter until melted and smooth, adding Italian seasoning if using. Let it cook for another 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened but not boiling.
Toss broccoli rabe with the sauce just before serving. Serve hot as a side dish or over cooked pasta.
Optional: Garnish with fresh herbs like parsley or basil and serve with crusty bread if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
